title: Benstonea stenocarpa
intro: Benstonea stenocarpa, also known as the "Australian Stoneseed", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia. The health benefits of Benstonea stenocarpa include its potential to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Benstonea stenocarpa is commonly prepared into teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, respiratory infections, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Benstonea stenocarpa is a species of flowering plant in the family Pandanaceae. It is native to New Guinea and belongs to the genus Benstonea. The genus Benstonea is a group of plants that are part of the family Pandanaceae, which is a family of tropical plants. The family Pandanaceae is part of the order Pandanales, a group of flowering plants. The order Pandanales is part of the angiosperm clade, which includes the majority of flowering plant species.
